name: Jellyfish Engineering Management Platform API
what_it_does: Engineering management platform with REST API — enabling VPs of Engineering and CTOs to align engineering work with business outcomes, measure DORA metrics, analyze investment allocation (features vs debt vs bugs), track delivery health, and correlate engineering activity with Jira/GitHub/GitLab data, with Sleuth DORA metrics integration (acquired 2023).
use_cases: Tracking engineering investment allocation via Jellyfish API for business alignment reporting
Measuring DORA metrics via Jellyfish API (powered by Sleuth acquisition) for delivery performance
Analyzing team delivery health via Jellyfish API for engineering leadership dashboards
Correlating Jira and GitHub data via Jellyfish API for work-to-value mapping
Generating engineering reports via Jellyfish API for board-level engineering communication
not_for: Individual developer performance reviews (Jellyfish focuses on team and business-level insights)
Real-time incident management (use PagerDuty for operational incidents)
Teams without GitHub/GitLab + Jira integration (requires both for full value)
best_when: A VP of Engineering or CTO needs to communicate engineering value to business stakeholders — Jellyfish translates Git/Jira activity into business outcomes like feature throughput and investment percentages.
avoid_when: You want Git-only analytics, individual metrics, or a simpler DORA dashboard without the business-alignment layer.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header or X-API-Key header. API key from Jellyfish account settings. REST API at api.jellyfish.co/v1/. Jira integration required for investment allocation data. GitHub/GitLab integration for DORA metrics (Sleuth engine). No OAuth2 for third-party access. Read-only analytics API.

pricing.notes: US company (Boston). Founded 2017 by Andrew Lau and Eric Knoop. $71M+ funding (Tiger Global, Accel, Bessemer). Acquired Sleuth (DORA metrics platform) 2023. Jellyfish's key value: 'investment allocation' — what percentage of engineering time goes to new features vs bugs vs tech debt vs infrastructure, mapped to business goals. Engineering Reports: automated exec-level summaries. Business Outcomes: map GitHub/Jira work to OKRs. Delivery Health: sprint velocity, planned vs actual. Integration depth: Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Linear, Azure DevOps. Used by: LendingClub, HubSpot, Mindbody. Engineering management (not developer tools) — CTO/VP audience.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer or X-API-Key. Sleuth DORA engine (acquisition). Jira + GitHub required. Read-only. Business alignment focus (not dev surveillance). $71M+ funded. US company. SOC2/GDPR. Annual contracts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All metric reads are idempotent — historical analytics data is deterministic. Investment allocation reads idempotent by team and date range. No write operations in standard API.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires BOTH Jira and GitHub/GitLab for full value — investment allocation needs issue tracking + code
Sleuth DORA engine acquired 2023 — Sleuth product may evolve under Jellyfish umbrella
Investment allocation is Jellyfish's core metric: % of time on features/bugs/debt/infra
Engineering management platform (CTO/VP audience) — not a developer productivity tool
Read-only API — data comes from integrations configured in the Jellyfish UI
US data residency — verify GDPR compliance for EU teams

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token or X-API-Key. SOC2 and GDPR certified. Engineering analytics data (investment allocation, delivery metrics) is sensitive business IP. GitHub/GitLab and Jira integration credentials must be secured. US data residency. Read-only analytics reduces breach impact. Sleuth acquisition: security posture continuity under Jellyfish.
